steps.title: Expand the given binomial product
steps.content:
We need to find the value of $p$ such that an expression containing $p$ is equivalent to $6x^2 - 155x + 24$. Start by expanding the product $(x - p)(6x - 113)$ using the distributive property.

$$( x - p)(6x - 113) = x \cdot 6x + x \cdot (-113) + (-p) \cdot 6x + (-p) \cdot (-113)$$
$$= 6x^2 - 113x - 6px + 113p$$
$$= 6x^2 - (113 + 6p)x + 113p$$

steps.title: Match the coefficient of x with the target expression
steps.content:
The expanded form is $6x^2 - (113 + 6p)x + 113p$. We want this (with a constant adjustment) to equal $6x^2 - 155x + 24$. Comparing the coefficients of $x$:

$$-(113 + 6p) = -155$$
$$113 + 6p = 155$$
$$6p = 42$$
$$p = 7$$

steps.title: Verify using the constant term
steps.content:
Check that $p = 7$ works with the constant term. When $p = 7$:

$(x - 7)(6x - 113) = 6x^2 - 155x + 113(7) = 6x^2 - 155x + 791$

To complete the equivalence to $6x^2 - 155x + 24$, we would subtract $791 - 24 = 767$, confirming the structure of the original expression. The fact that matching the $x$ coefficient uniquely determines $p = 7$ means this is our answer. That matches choice B — B is correct.
